title: Sec. 47-512. - Cleaning and maintenance requirements. (Houston Code of Ordinances, Ch. 47, Art. XI, Div. 9)
excerpt: Ch. 47 WATER AND SEWERS > Art. XI TRANSPORTATION AND TREATMENT OF CERTAIN WASTES > Div. 9 GENERATION, TRANSFER AND DISPOSAL OF WASTE > Subdiv. 1 Generator Responsibilities. Sec. 47-512(b): 'Each interceptor or holding tank shall be fully evacuated at least once every 90 days.' Sec. 47-512(c)(1): pump more frequently when 'Twenty-five percent or more of the wetted height of the grease trap or interceptor, as measured from the bottom of the device to the invert of the outlet pipe, contains floating materials, sediment, oils or greases.' Sec. 47-512(f): full evacuation/clean/inspect at least once every 180 days. Last amended Ord. No. 2020-1024, 12-2-2020.
query: Houston grease trap ordinance Municode Sec. 47-512

title: Special Waste Generators | Houston Health Department
excerpt: Official Houston Health Department page: 'Section 47-512(b) of the City of Houston Code of Ordinances mandates that every interceptor located within the incorporated City limits must be fully evacuated at least on a quarterly basis (every 90 days) unless a Notice of Waiver application is submitted and approved.' Links directly to Municode Ch. 47, Article XI. Notes proposed Chapter 47 changes reducing O/G limit from 400 to 200 mg/L. Citation fines $250-$2000 per violation under Ch. 47 Art. XI.

title: Grease Trap Regulations and Maintenance in Houston | Houston Plumbing Authority
excerpt: Secondary corroboration that City of Houston grease interceptor requirements are 'administered primarily under the Houston Code of Ordinances, Chapter 47' (Water and Sewers), enforced by Houston Public Works/Health Dept. Links to Municode Ch. 47 node COOR_CH47UT. AI-generated reference; used only as corroboration of the Chapter 47 framing, not for specific numbers.

title: Understanding Grease Removal And The 25% Rule - City of Dallas
excerpt: The Dallas City Code was amended in October 2007 and requires all grease traps and interceptors be pumped out at least every 90 days or when it is 25% full of solids and grease. Per City Ordinance ... maintained ... when they reach 25% of the design capacity OR a minimum of quarterly.

title: Fats, Oils and Grease Ordinance (San Antonio City Code Chapter 34, Div. 5) - San Antonio Water System
excerpt: Sec. 34-527(b) Frequency: evacuate ... at a frequency not less often than every ninety (90) days and within two (2) working days whenever twenty-five (25) percent or more of the wetted height of the interceptor, measured from the bottom of the device to the invert of the outlet pipe, contains floating materials, sediment, fats, oils, or grease. Records retained 5 years (34-528).

title: Fats, Oils & Grease Program Annual Inspection - San Antonio Water System
excerpt: Per Chapter 34 Article 5 Division 4 Sec 34-527(d) ... Not less frequently than once per calendar year ... inspect each interceptor. All defects shall be corrected ... within ninety (90) days of each inspection.

title: Grease Trap Maintenance | Austin Water | AustinTexas.gov
excerpt: Grease traps must be pumped out at least once every 90 days, or sooner if grease and solids make up 50% or more of the wetted height of the trap as measured from the bottom of the trap to the invert of the outlet pipe (§ 15-10-197). Manifests kept 3 years (§ 15-10-165); FOG limit 200 mg/L (§ 15-10-23).

title: 30 Tex. Admin. Code § 312.142 - Transporter Registration
publisher: Cornell Law School - Legal Information Institute (LII), reproducing Texas Administrative Code
excerpt: (a) Persons who plan to transport biosolids/sewage sludge, water treatment residuals, domestic septage, chemical toilet waste, grit trap waste, or grease trap waste regulated under this subchapter shall apply for registration with the commission on forms furnished by the executive director and receive a registration from the executive director prior to commencing operations. (c) Persons who apply ... shall maintain a current copy of the registration authorization, as annotated by the executive director with an assigned registration number, at their designated place of business and in each vehicle operated under that registration. (d) The expiration date of the registration shall be August 31st of the year in which it expires. Registrations are required to be renewed biennially.
query: 30 TAC 312.142 transporter registration grease grit trap

title: 30 Tex. Admin. Code § 312.145 - Transporters - Recordkeeping (trip ticket)
publisher: Cornell Law School - Legal Information Institute (LII), reproducing Texas Administrative Code
excerpt: (a) Persons who collect and transport waste subject to control under this subchapter shall maintain a record of each individual collection and deposit. Such records must be in the form of a trip ticket. Required fields: (1) name, address, telephone number, and commission registration number of transporter; (2) name, signature (or electronic signature), address, and telephone number of the person who generated the waste and the date collected; (3) type and amount(s) of waste collected or transported; (4) name and signature(s) of responsible person(s) collecting, transporting, and depositing the waste; (5) date and place where the waste was deposited; (6) identification (permit or site registration number, location, and operator) of the facility where the waste was deposited; (7) name and signature (or electronic signature) of facility on-site representative acknowledging receipt of the waste and the amount of waste received; (8) the volume of the grease and grit trap or the septic tank. (b) Trip tickets must be divided into five parts ... One copy returned by the transporter to the generator within 15 days after the waste is received at the disposal or processing facility ... Copies of trip tickets must be retained for five years and be readily available for review by commission staff or be submitted to the executive director upon request. By July 1st, transporters must submit an annual summary of activities for the previous period of June 1st through May 31st.

title: Waste from Grease Traps, Grit Traps, and Septic Tanks: Questions and Answers (TCEQ RG-389)
excerpt: Only transporters registered with the TCEQ can legally pump and transport grease-trap waste. All liquid waste—including grease-trap, grit-trap, and septic-tank waste—must be transported by registered haulers. Trip ticket / multi-part manifest required fields listed for Generator (name/address/phone, type of waste, trap/tank capacity, amount, date, signature), Transporter (name/address/phone, transporter's TCEQ registration number, optional city/county permit number, gallons transported, driver name & signature), Receiving Facility (name & TCEQ permit/registration number, address, date, amount received, site rep signature). 'In order to verify your transporter's TCEQ registration, ask to see a copy and check for a dated sticker on the truck's door. Each vehicle registered with the TCEQ is required to have both.' All transporters must meet the requirements in 30 TAC 312.
query: TCEQ RG-389 grease grit trap transporter trip ticket

title: Transporting Waste in Texas - A Guide to Regulations (TCEQ RG-086)
publisher: Texas Commission on Environmental Quality (TCEQ), via UNT Portal to Texas History
excerpt: The TCEQ regulates municipal wastewater treatment plant sludge, water treatment plant sludge, grit and grease trap waste, and septic tank and chemical toilet waste (30 TAC 312 Subchapter G). If you haul these types of waste, please see The Step-by-Step Guide to Sludge Transporter Registration and Operating Requirements, TCEQ Publication RG-309. To register as a transporter of one of these wastes, call the TCEQ at 512-239-3695. [NOTE: this 2014 guidance labels the subchapter 'G'; the rule sections 312.142/312.145 are within Subchapter F of the current TAC.]
query: TCEQ transporting waste guide 30 TAC 312 subchapter

title: City of Early, TX Code of Ordinances Ch. 22 Art. V (Grease Traps) - Transporter definition citing 30 TAC 312.142
publisher: City of Early, Texas (municipal code, EncodePlus)
excerpt: Transporter means a person who is registered with and authorized by the TCEQ to transport sewage sludge, water treatment sludge, domestic septage, chemical, toilet waste, grit trap waste, or grease trap waste in accordance with 30, Texas Administrative Code § 312.142.
query: corroborate 30 TAC 312.142 transporter registration grease grit trap

title: Pretreatment Program FAQs — City of Dallas Water Utilities
excerpt: How often must my grease trap be cleaned? The Dallas City Code, Chapter 19, Sec 19-126.2 (c) specifies all grease traps must be completely emptied and cleaned by a licensed hauler. The interceptors must be cleaned as often as necessary but not less than every 90 days, or whenever 25% or more of the interceptor contains floating materials, sediment, oils, or grease.
query: Dallas City Code Chapter 19 grease interceptor cleaning 25 percent 90 days section
